package service

import (
	"context"
	"fmt"
	"strconv"

	"github.com/gin-gonic/gin"
)

// Positive scenario for service_method_handles_pagination_or_query_parsing:
// service parses page, sort, filter, or URL values that should already be
// normalized at the transport boundary.
type ProductService struct {
	repo ProductRepository
}

type ProductRepository interface {
	List(ctx context.Context, offset, limit int, sortBy string) ([]Product, error)
}

type Product struct {
	ID    string
	Name  string
	Price int
}

func NewProductService(repo ProductRepository) *ProductService {
	return &ProductService{repo: repo}
}

func (s *ProductService) ListProducts(c *gin.Context) error {
	page, err := strconv.Atoi(c.DefaultQuery("page", "1"))
	if err != nil || page < 1 {
		c.JSON(400, gin.H{"error": "invalid page parameter"})
		return fmt.Errorf("invalid page: %w", err)
	}

	size, err := strconv.Atoi(c.DefaultQuery("size", "20"))
	if err != nil || size < 1 || size > 100 {
		c.JSON(400, gin.H{"error": "invalid size parameter"})
		return fmt.Errorf("invalid size: %w", err)
	}

	sortBy := c.DefaultQuery("sort", "created_at")
	filter := c.Query("filter")

	offset := (page - 1) * size

	products, err := s.repo.List(c.Request.Context(), offset, size, sortBy)
	if err != nil {
		c.JSON(500, gin.H{"error": err.Error()})
		return err
	}

	_ = filter

	c.JSON(200, gin.H{
		"products": products,
		"page":     page,
		"size":     size,
		"sort":     sortBy,
	})
	return nil
}
